title: Kemal Özkırşehirli
description: Academic homepage for Kemal Özkırşehirli: AI/ML for science, order-agnostic 3D mesh generation, computer-aided drug discovery, protein and genome modeling, molecular simulation, statistical mechanics, and auditable scientific workflow engineering.

author.name: Kemal Özkırşehirli
author.role: B.Sc. candidate at MIT · GPA 5.0/5.0 · Majors in Computer Science and Engineering, Physics, and Mathematics · Minors in Philosophy and Writing
author.bio: I am an international student from İstanbul, Türkiye, and a B.Sc. candidate at MIT with a 5.0/5.0 GPA, majoring in Computer Science and Engineering, Physics, and Mathematics, with minors in Philosophy and Writing. I use AI/ML frameworks, generative and diffusion models, free-energy methods, atomistic molecular dynamics, Monte Carlo, density-functional theory, inference, and robust optimization to convert complex scientific systems into parsable, computable representations and extract governing rules for scientific decision-making.
highlights: Current work spans MeshAnyOrder order-agnostic 3D mesh generation, TBXT/brachyury small-molecule discovery, PRISM protein-model red-teaming, ChromoGen-Engine V2, A*STAR V2M autoimmune target discovery, ChemAgent-QSM, EVEdesign, antibody RL, Pedal AI chemistry-agent infrastructure, and ML-augmented renormalization group theory.

Research and engineering across the Özkırşehirli Group, MIT Chemistry, MIT CSAIL, MIT Physics, A*STAR EDDC, PRISM/W2D2/Siemens, Harvard Medical School, Columbia, AI@MIT/AIM Labs, Weizmann Institute, Pedal AI, Sepal AI/Mercor, and the Buchwald Lab.

Medalist at IChO and IMChO; TÜBİTAK National Chemistry Olympiad silver medalist; Columbia Science Research Fellow; World Science Scholar; AIME qualifier; AlgoTrade Hackathon 2026 selectee; and recipient of Waterloo CHEM13, Galois, and Sir Isaac Newton distinctions.

selectedSections.title: Molecular Simulation, CADD, and Chemical Physics

items.title: TBXT
items.description: 11-person chordoma/TBXT hit-identification program targeting PDB 6F59 site F; 2,274 prior-art compounds, 737 analogs, 30,000 BRICS recombinations, a 570-compound novelty-filtered pool, and a docking/QSAR/GNINA/Boltz-2/MMGBSA-FEP/selectivity funnel

items.title: ChemAgent-QSM
items.description: LLM-guided PySCF pipelines for electronic structure, optimized geometries, vibrational spectra, time-correlation functions, local order, relaxation, mobility, and dynamical-heterogeneity analysis

items.title: Renormalization Group
items.description: PyTorch/GNN-augmented Kadanoff renormalization-group framework for fixed-point discovery and phase-boundary mapping in spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic systems

items.title: Buchwald Lab
items.description: CuH-catalyst development for asymmetric methylation and alkylation of vinyl boronate esters, with 1H-NMR, chiral-HPLC, substrate-scope expansion, and multi-gram scale-up

items.title: Pedal AI
items.description: Python/LangGraph and FastAPI infrastructure for chemistry agents, molecular-prediction engines, inspectable retrosynthesis trees, provenance, and decision-ready synthesis workflows

items.title: Kupcinet-Getz
items.description: PINN- and neural-ODE-enhanced stochastic biochemical reaction-diffusion, custom Euler-Maruyama, Runge-Kutta, and Gillespie SSA solvers, and trajectory-level GNN/transformer analysis

items.title: Sepal AI / Mercor
items.description: Graduate-level chemistry LLM data, retrosynthesis and reaction-mechanism tasks, chemical-validity rubrics, prompt analysis, and failure-mode annotation

items.title: DFT → kMC
items.description: Automated multi-scale computational-organic-chemistry pipeline linking DFT transition-state analysis to stochastic kinetic Monte Carlo across solvent pathways

selectedSections.title: Biomolecular AI, Protein, and Genome Modeling

items.title: MeshAnyOrder
items.description: Order-agnostic autoregressive transformer for point-cloud-conditioned 3D mesh generation with quantized face tokens, arbitrary traversal seeds, 3D RoPE, triangle/quad tokenization, topology-aware constraints, frontier-parallel decoding, and publication-grade ablations

items.title: PRISM
items.description: Adversarial evaluation of protein foundation models across biological plausibility, sequence-structure-function consistency, developability, uncertainty calibration, claimed mechanisms, and failure taxonomies

items.title: ChromoGen V2
items.description: Conditional diffusion for small-molecule-perturbed single-cell 3D chromatin, integrating Plate-C / Easy Dip-C structures with CHARM-style same-cell multi-omics and leakage-aware evaluation

items.title: V2M
items.description: Calibrated genetics-to-mechanism autoimmune target discovery using single-cell foundation models, QTL/colocalization, retrodiction benchmarks, perturbation validation, and structure-aware prioritization

items.title: EVEdesign
items.description: Open-source, method-independent protein design across a 21-person, 18-institution, 8-country collaboration, with uncertainty-aware multi-objective candidate selection and lab-in-the-loop workflows

items.title: Antibody RL
items.description: Six-person structure-informed cross-attention transformer and ESM-2 pipeline with OAS, SAbDab, and IEDB data, PPO against AF-Multimer/IGFold metrics, and distributed training on MIT SuperCloud

items.title: Stack
items.description: Python, C/C++, CUDA, PyTorch, Transformers, GNNs, HPC/GPU programming, Linux, Bash, Git, SLURM/PBS/qsub, RDKit, OpenMM/OpenFE, PySCF, LangGraph, FastAPI, and LaTeX

items.description: 8.592[J] Statistical Physics in Biology; 2.094[J] Atomistic Modeling and Simulation of Materials and Structures; 8.05 Quantum Physics II; 5.601 Thermodynamics I; 5.351 Fundamentals of Spectroscopy; 5.43 Advanced Organic Chemistry

items.title: CS/AI/Systems

profileLines: B.Sc. candidate at MIT · GPA 5.0/5.0 · Majors in Computer Science and Engineering, Physics, and Mathematics · Minors in Philosophy and Writing
Research across MIT Chemistry, MIT CSAIL, MIT Physics, A*STAR EDDC, PRISM/W2D2, and Harvard Medical School
International student from İstanbul, Türkiye · expected May 2027

home.researchStatement: My work and the Özkırşehirli Group aim to make difficult scientific systems parsable, computable, and decision-ready. (1) We develop MeshAnyOrder, an order-agnostic autoregressive transformer for topology-aware 3D mesh generation from point clouds. (2) We build multi-stage CADD pipelines for TBXT/brachyury, integrating docking, QSAR, CNN rescoring, co-folding, free-energy scaffolding, selectivity, and sourceability. (3) I develop and evaluate generative, foundation, and agentic systems for protein design, chromatin organization, autoimmune target discovery, quantum/statistical mechanics, and antibody discovery. (4) I use molecular simulation, stochastic processes, and robust optimization to extract governing rules and create auditable scientific workflows.

group.description: A student-led organization of researchers founded and led by Kemal Özkırşehirli.
